Physical Intelligence.

What happens when the nation's network learns to act in the physical world. Told in five acts: from one conviction to a grid in the sky.

01
Act I · Who we are

Who is e&,and why physical AI?

Our vision

National operator and trusted enabler for government and enterprises driving autonomous innovation, accelerating the UAE’s vision through leading capabilities and partnerships.

Our mission

e& is building the unified, sovereign operating fabric that turns physical AI from a capability claim into a national service: intelligent assets, Edge AI, the largest 5.5G network in the UAE and a sovereign data platform, powering autonomous missions across drones, robotics and connected autonomous vehicles.

Why physical AI, and why us

Drones are the first physical AI body to scale. Regulation is live, hardware is proven and every industry has missions waiting: the fastest route from AI capability to national service.

They run on what e& already owns. Every flight hour consumes towers, 5.5G, edge compute and sovereign cloud. No other UAE operator brings that substrate.

The fabric is already in place, built on a decade of drone operations in the Gulf. Orchestration, connectivity, approvals and data residency are solved. The question is no longer can this work, but which use case we activate first.

Unified orchestration·Accelerating UAE autonomy·Sovereign by design
01
Act I · The vision

Building the region’smost advanced physical AI.

Machines that fly, walk, climb and dive, operated as one national capability. It stands on four building blocks:

01

Advanced drone network

A nationwide grid of autonomous docks on e& tower sites. BVLOS, around the clock, under sovereign UTM.

02

Physical intelligence labs

Where platforms, payloads and AI models are benchmarked, hardened and certified before national service.

03

Sovereign operating fabric

One control plane, one data spine, on sovereign cloud. Planned, flown, logged and billed in country.

04

Air, land & sea

One multi dimensional offering: drones, robots, crawlers and ROVs on one platform, under one SLA.

Four building blocks. One operator. The capability the region will measure itself against.

01
Act I · The right to play

Why e&.The right to play.

Physical AI at national scale is not bought, it is operated. Five assets give e& the right to play, and every one of them is live today, not on a roadmap.

01

Sovereign orchestration

UAE sovereign UTM: command, mission dispatch, telemetry and deconfliction, aligned with the Cybersecurity Council. Certified, live and operational, with full data residency.

02

The largest 5.5G network

Nationwide fiber and 5.5G in deployment: real time telemetry and heavy sensor payloads move over the radio, everywhere missions fly.

03

Physical AI assets

Drones, docks, robots and fleet management already owned and operating. Same quarter deployment, not an 18 month vendor build.

04

Edge AI

Inference at the device and the MEC layer: defects, deviations and alerts resolved at the edge, without round tripping to the cloud.

05

Customer & ecosystem access

Every UAE government body and enterprise is already an e& customer, with global technology partners behind us. Day one contracting, day one integration.

Any one of these can be bought. All five together cannot. That is the difference between selling drones and operating national infrastructure.

02
Act II · The tower play

Every tower becomesa sensor fusion site.

On the mast: counter-UAS sensing. Passive RF and radar detect uncooperative drones and feed every track into the Sovereign UTM picture.

At the base: a resident fleet. A docked drone covering 3 to 7 km, and a ground robot for on site inspection rounds.

Beside it: MEC edge compute. Detection, defect and anomaly models run where the data is captured, not in a distant cloud.

Already on site: power, fiber, 5.5G radio and secured land. Roughly 80% of the site works exist before we begin.

One site visit turns a telecom tower into a node of national physical intelligence.

CUAS · RF + RADAR 5.5G RADIO UNAUTHORISED UAS
DETECTED · TRACKED DRONE DOCK · 3–7 KM GROUND ROBOT MEC EDGE COMPUTE
02
Act II · The sovereign stack

Seven layers.One prime: e&.

The reference architecture behind every mission: best of breed partner platforms, integrated behind one e& control plane, on sovereign cloud, under one SLA.

Sovereign by design
L7

User touchpoint

Self service portal, orders & workflow, billing on e& enterprise systems.

L6

Mission command & control

Flight planning, fleet & pilot management at 4 to 8 drones per operator, flight logs, live video, GIS decision support.

L5

AI & analytics

Real time inference at the edge, per use case models, continuous training on missions flown here.

L4

Data lakehouse

Telemetry pipeline, structured and unstructured storage, analytics and reporting: e& owned, UAE resident.

e& DataOps
L3

Security & managed services

Drone identity & tracking, NOC/SOC, and certified Sovereign UTM integration with national airspace.

Sovereign UTMe& SOC
L2

Connectivity

5G RAN and core, 5.5G in deployment, RF mesh for site BVLOS, nationwide fiber backhaul.

e& 5.5G
L1

Physical fleet & sites

COTS drones, docks, quadrupeds and command vehicles, installed and maintained on the e& tower estate.

The ecosystem behind the stack
02

Anyone can buy a drone. Almost no one can do what we can.

This is not a strategy a competitor can copy. It is a position we already hold.

03
Act III · The network

One networkwatching the grid.

Docks anchored on e& tower sites. Power, connectivity and land are already there: roughly 80% of site works solved before the first pour.

3 to 7 km of autonomous coverage per dock. Coverage scales with the existing national footprint, not with new construction.

Sovereign UTM overhead. Every mission dispatched, deconflicted and logged in country.

03
Act III · How we operate

Fixed. Mobile. Crewed.The telecom playbook.

The pattern that built every national network, applied to autonomous robotics: a fixed grid, a mobile fleet and an expert field force.

UBIQUITY
01

Tower mounted docks

Every tower becomes a launchpad
3–7 KM

A national grid of always on drone docks installed on existing e& tower infrastructure. Routine patrol, scheduled inspection and rapid response, without a crew on site.

ELASTICITY
02

Mobile command vehicles

Physical intelligence on wheels
ON STATION IN HOURS

Mission equipped vehicles with onboard drones, edge compute and uplink. Deployed where the work is: events, project sites, remote areas, emergencies.

PRECISION
03

Expert crewed operations

The specialist field force
CONFINED · HIGH RISK

Certified pilots and mission specialists fly what autonomy cannot yet: confined space entry, live flare inspection, high risk industrial work. Same platform, same data flow, same SLA.

One control plane·OEM agnostic·Full UAE data residency·One SLA
04
Act IV · Robotics

Robots where humansshould not go.

The anchor mission: ATEX Zone 1 process inspection. Certified legged robots patrol explosive atmospheres autonomously, so people never have to enter them.

Detect leaks. Hydrocarbon and toxic gas caught by acoustic imaging and point sensors, on every patrol, in Zone 1.

See heat. Thermal anomalies on vessels, flanges and rotating equipment flagged before they become failures.

Read everything. Automated gauge reads and visual checks, logged and trended on every round, with zero human exposure.

50+ OEM platforms benchmarked. The best of them deployed, under one orchestration layer.

ATEX Zone 1 Gas leak detected Gauge read · logged ATEX certified quadruped
ANYmal X·ANYmal D·Spot·Taurob·Magnetic crawlers·Subsea & hull
04
Act IV · Subsea

Below thewaterline.

ROV services: tethered underwater vehicles inspecting the assets divers should not be sent into, on the same platform as every aerial mission.

Ports & jetties. Quay walls, piles, breakwaters and moorings surveyed for scour, cracking and marine growth without closing the berth.

Hulls & structures. Vessel hulls, offshore assets and bridge piers inspected in hours, with sonar where visibility drops to zero.

Dams & water assets. Reservoirs, intakes and treatment tanks inspected in service: no dewatering, no dive team, no shutdown.

One data spine. Subsea footage, sonar and laser scaling land in the same sovereign platform, next to the aerial record of the same asset.

Tethered ROV Pile scour · cracks 100-300 m depth class
Quay walls·Jetty piles·Hulls·Dams & intakes·Sonar & laser scaling·Search & recovery
04
Act IV · Confined space

Insidethe asset.

Tanks, boilers, vessels, stacks and pipelines inspected from within by caged drones and specialised crawler robots: no human entry, no scaffolding.

Caged inspection drones. Collision tolerant airframes with LiDAR SLAM fly inside tanks and boilers, mapping as they go.

Magnetic UT crawlers. Wall thickness surveys on shells and pipelines to API 653 and 570, robotically, on live assets.

Internal pipeline crawlers. Kilometre class runs through lines from 8 inches up, where no drone and no person can go.

Days, not weeks. No entry permits, no scaffolding crews, no gas freeing marathons: the asset returns to service faster, with a richer record.

Caged LiDAR drone · SLAM map UT wall thickness · API 653 Internal pipeline crawler No human entry
Storage tanks·Boilers & vessels·Stacks·Pipelines·Sewers·UT & visual NDT

Act VI · De-risked

Already real.Already funded.

UTM certified & live

The sovereign traffic management layer is operational today, flying scheduled BVLOS missions over national rail infrastructure.

Robots in service

The robotics MVP went from kickoff to operating robots in 60 days, with trained operators and documented procedures.

Regulatory cover

Operations run inside UAE frameworks, with e& at the table where the rules for autonomous airspace are written.

Board level commitment

A financed, decade long national build out, not a pilot budget. The fleet, the docks and the models are already funded to scale.

06
Act VI · The path

Prove. Build.Operate.

One path from first flight to national utility. Each step earns the next.

Weeks

01 · Prove

A scoped pilot on your highest value use case. Real missions on your sites, measured against your KPIs. Weeks, not quarters.

Months

02 · Build the corridors

Docks and flight corridors across your estate, robots on your process units, feeds integrated into your control rooms.

Years

03 · Operate the grid

An always on service, contracted for years. Coverage, autonomy and reporting that improve every quarter, without a new project.

The buyer does not run a project. It switches on a utility.

06
Act VI · The decision

Choose howyou consume it.

Subscribe to results, not robots. OpEx replaces CapEx: you do not buy aircraft, pilots or platforms. You buy verified outcomes with an SLA.

Shared

Subscription on the shared grid

Always on coverage from docks serving up to 5 customers per site, around 10 flights per day per dock today, growing with every drone generation. Patrols, alerts and reporting included.

Dedicated

Full site capacity, one customer

For persistent surveillance, traffic and security operations: dedicated fleets, command vehicles and operators integrated into your control room, under your mission priorities.

Per use

Pay for the outcome, one mission at a time

Per inspection, per km of corridor, per verified report. The public drone network model as the grid reaches critical mass across entire cities.

Every construct is a service: e& owns, operates, insures and upgrades the hardware. Start in one construct, blend as you scale.

06
Act VI · The ask

The first missionis yours to pick.

We are not asking for a transformation program. We are asking for one mission that matters to you.

01

Pick the use case

One high value mission where minutes, access or coverage hurt today: inspection, surveillance or response.

02

Scope it together

A half day workshop: sites, KPIs, data owners and flight approvals, mapped onto the grid that already exists.

03

Fly it

A verified mission on your site, measured against your KPIs, delivered as a service from day one.

to start

verified mission

required

One workshop. One corridor. A verified mission inside ninety days.

Application · Construction monitoring

From raw land
to handover.

Six productised use cases cover the full build: outdoors and indoors, progress and quality, payment evidence and the digital twin, all on the same sovereign platform.

Survey grade, in a day

Survey & volumetrics

PPK/RTK drone photogrammetry and LiDAR produce survey grade topography and earthwork volumes in a fraction of the time of ground crews, with an auditable comparison against the design.

Cut / fill volumes to ±2% accuracy.

Bare earth DTM from LiDAR, through vegetation.

Boundary, drainage and slope risk analysis.

As built vs design deviation maps.

PPK / RTK2 cm GSD±2% volumesLiDAR DTM
See every site, every week

Construction progress monitoring

Regular autonomous overflights are turned by AI into orthomosaics and 3D models, then compared automatically against the construction schedule and the BIM model, zone by zone, week by week.

Progress % by zone, measured, not estimated.

BIM to point cloud comparison and clash flags.

Schedule slippage alerts before they compound.

A verifiable, time stamped as built record.

2 cm GSDWeekly cadenceBIM overlaySovereign cloud
Every floor, every two weeks

Indoor reality capture

360° cameras, wearable scanners, LiDAR and ground robots capture every floor on a biweekly cadence, each scan auto aligned to the BIM model and floor plans: an immutable, time stamped record of the whole build.

Biweekly 360° / LiDAR capture by floor, zone and trade.

Auto registration to BIM, drawings and coordinates.

Pre concealment scan gate before ceiling and wall closure.

Immutable record across the life cycle.

360° + LiDARBiweeklyBIM aligned50°C rated
Catch it before it is concealed

AI quality & snagging

AI scans every capture for defects, snags and deviations, classifying each finding by severity, trade, package and location, with a visual basis of detection before it ever reaches the reviewer queue.

Auto classified findings: severity, trade, package, BOQ line.

MEP findings grouped by system: HVAC, electrical, fire, BMS.

Snagging, NCR and hold point workflows with audit trail.

Trend analysis and predictive defect hotspots per package.

Explainable AI<15% false positives48h SLAMEP grouping
Progress measured, not claimed

BIM progress & payment evidence

AI measures actual physical progress against the Primavera P6 schedule and the 4D BIM model, by package, building, floor and zone, and links verified progress to BOQ and payment milestones as advisory evidence packs.

Planned vs actual by WBS activity, with slippage alerts.

4D BIM comparison and visual progress heatmaps per floor.

Payment validation evidence packs for PMC and consultants.

Overclaim and missing evidence flags before each cycle.

P6 + WBS4D BIMHeatmapsAI advisory only
No surprise operations, day one

As built & digital twin

Field verified capture becomes a LOD 300-350 as built model with structured COBie asset data, published straight into the client’s Autodesk Construction Cloud: a BIM linked digital twin ready for FM from day one of occupancy.

Verified as built model at the LOD defined in the AIR.

COBie asset data for all maintainable MEP components.

Open standard delivery into ACC: IFC / Revit, ISO 19650.

Documented integration pathway to FM and CAFM platforms.

LOD 300-350COBie + IFCACC deliveryISO 19650
